---
title: "Natural Language Processing with Disaster Tweets"
author:
  - name: Chris Endemann
    email: endemann@wisc.edu

date: 2024-09-12
date-format: long
image: "../../images/DiasterTweets.png"

categories:
  - Projects
  - ML Marathon
  - MLM24
  - NLP
  - Classification
  - Deep learning


---

The Disaster Tweets challenge is a beginner-friendly NLP competition featured in the [2024 Machine Learning Marathon (MLM24)](https://ml-marathon.wisc.edu/). Participants build models to classify whether tweets are about real disasters or not -- an accessible introduction to natural language processing and text classification.

### Challenge design
- **Task**: Predict whether a given tweet is about a real disaster (1) or not (0).
- **Domain**: Natural language processing -- text classification with real-world social media data.
- **Methods**: Approaches range from TF-IDF + logistic regression to fine-tuned transformer models (BERT, DistilBERT).

### Links
- **Kaggle challenge**: [Natural Language Processing with Disaster Tweets](https://www.kaggle.com/c/nlp-getting-started)

## Related resources
- [**Workshop**: Intro to Natural Language Processing (NLP)](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-TextAnalysis_Python.html): Brush up on NLP basics before tackling text classification challenges.
- [**Library**: Hugging Face](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Toolbox/Libraries/HuggingFace.html): Fine-tuning transformer models like BERT and DistilBERT is a top approach — learn how to find and use them on Hugging Face.
- [**Workshop**: Intro to Deep Learning with PyTorch](https://uw-madison-datascience.github.io/ML-X-Nexus/Learn/Workshops/Intro-Deeplearning_PyTorch.html): Learn deep learning fundamentals for building text classification models beyond TF-IDF baselines.

## Questions
If you have any lingering questions about this project, please feel free to post to the [Nexus Q&A](https://github.com/UW-Madison-DataScience/ML-X-Nexus/discussions/categories/q-a) on GitHub. We will improve materials on this website as additional questions come in.
